project.name: Tars
project.description: Interactive 3D multi-leg gravity-assist trajectory optimizer for a space science course.

nodes.name: .env.example
nodes.summary: Documents the environment variables used by the API, worker, Redis connection, ephemeris cache, scene scaling, and server runtime.

nodes.name: docker-compose.yml
nodes.summary: Orchestrates Redis, the FastAPI API service, the ARQ worker, and an nginx-hosted frontend with shared ephemeris cache volumes.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Builds the Python API container, installs scientific and web dependencies, compiles the trajectory protobuf schema, and starts the FastAPI server with uvicorn.
nodes.languageNotes: The image compiles protobuf stubs during build and sets PYTHONPATH so top-level src imports work.

nodes.name: style.css
nodes.summary: Defines the dark space-themed frontend layout, controls, panels, and animation styling for the Three.js trajectory interface.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Provides the browser UI shell, controls, status panels, and script imports for the Three.js trajectory optimizer frontend.

nodes.name: app.js
nodes.summary: Implements the interactive Three.js solar-system scene, API calls, WebSocket streaming, route planning, trajectory optimization, and mission visualization workflows.
nodes.languageNotes: Browser ES modules coordinate Three.js rendering with REST and WebSocket APIs.

nodes.name: planetData.js
nodes.summary: Provides frontend metadata for body display, texture mapping, rotation periods, visual radii, and descriptive planet information.

nodes.name: dateUtils.js
nodes.summary: Provides Julian-date, Unix-time, and nearest-sample helpers used by the frontend timeline and ephemeris interpolation code.

nodes.name: patch_engine.py
nodes.summary: Applies source patch operations through a small regex-based patch helper.
nodes.languageNotes: Python modules use FastAPI, async I/O, NumPy/SciPy, and Numba where performance matters.

nodes.name: patch_routes.py
nodes.summary: Contains route patching support used to adjust API route definitions.
nodes.languageNotes: Python modules use FastAPI, async I/O, NumPy/SciPy, and Numba where performance matters.

nodes.name: trajectory.proto
nodes.summary: Defines protobuf messages for vectors, trajectories, planetary snapshots, optimization progress updates, and porkchop plot payloads.
nodes.languageNotes: The protobuf schema is the cross-language binary contract for trajectory and streaming payloads.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Introduces the interplanetary trajectory optimizer, its Docker-based startup flow, three-layer architecture, optimizer behavior, scene coordinate mapping, and API reference.

nodes.name: requirements.txt
nodes.summary: Lists runtime Python dependencies for the FastAPI service, scientific optimization stack, async HTTP client, Redis task queue, and protobuf serialization.

nodes.name: fetch_ephemeris.py
nodes.summary: Provides a command-line utility that prewarms or refreshes the ephemeris spline cache from JPL Horizons data.
nodes.languageNotes: Python modules use FastAPI, async I/O, NumPy/SciPy, and Numba where performance matters.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Marks the source directory as a Python package for application modules.
nodes.languageNotes: Python modules use FastAPI, async I/O, NumPy/SciPy, and Numba where performance matters.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Marks the API routes directory as a Python package.

nodes.name: routes_http.py
nodes.summary: Defines HTTP endpoints, request and response models, validation, ephemeris queries, Lambert transfers, porkchop data, optimization job submission, and route planning.
nodes.languageNotes: Python modules use FastAPI, async I/O, NumPy/SciPy, and Numba where performance matters.

nodes.name: routes_ws.py
nodes.summary: Defines WebSocket endpoints for optimization progress streaming and live ephemeris snapshots.
nodes.languageNotes: Python modules use FastAPI, async I/O, NumPy/SciPy, and Numba where performance matters.

nodes.name: config.py
nodes.summary: Centralizes runtime settings for Redis, ephemeris cache range, scene scaling, and server host configuration.
nodes.languageNotes: Python modules use FastAPI, async I/O, NumPy/SciPy, and Numba where performance matters.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Marks the ephemeris package containing body metadata, Horizons access, and spline caching.

nodes.name: bodies.py
nodes.summary: Defines celestial body constants, NAIF identifiers, gravitational parameters, visual metadata, and lookup maps for planets, moons, and dwarf bodies.
nodes.languageNotes: Python modules use FastAPI, async I/O, NumPy/SciPy, and Numba where performance matters.

nodes.name: horizons_client.py
nodes.summary: Fetches and parses vector ephemeris data from the JPL Horizons API using async HTTP requests.
nodes.languageNotes: Python modules use FastAPI, async I/O, NumPy/SciPy, and Numba where performance matters.

nodes.name: spline_cache.py
nodes.summary: Builds and serves an in-memory cubic-spline ephemeris cache, including persistence, parent-relative moon handling, and date-range validation.
nodes.languageNotes: Python modules use FastAPI, async I/O, NumPy/SciPy, and Numba where performance matters.

nodes.name: main.py
nodes.summary: Creates the FastAPI application, configures CORS, warms the ephemeris cache during lifespan startup, and mounts HTTP and WebSocket routers.
nodes.languageNotes: Python modules use FastAPI, async I/O, NumPy/SciPy, and Numba where performance matters.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Marks the mechanics package containing orbital mechanics, Lambert solving, transforms, and multi-leg trajectory routines.

nodes.name: kepler.py
nodes.summary: Implements Keplerian element conversion, orbit geometry, and propagation routines used to render and compare orbital paths.
nodes.languageNotes: Python modules use FastAPI, async I/O, NumPy/SciPy, and Numba where performance matters.

nodes.name: lambert.py
nodes.summary: Implements a universal-variable Lambert solver and transfer delta-v calculation for two-body transfer arcs.
nodes.languageNotes: Python modules use FastAPI, async I/O, NumPy/SciPy, and Numba where performance matters.

nodes.name: multileg.py
nodes.summary: Computes chained multi-leg trajectories with Lambert arcs, flyby geometry, timing, C3 constraints, and serialization helpers.
nodes.languageNotes: Python modules use FastAPI, async I/O, NumPy/SciPy, and Numba where performance matters.

nodes.name: transforms.py
nodes.summary: Provides Julian-date conversion, distance scaling, coordinate transforms, and ecliptic-to-scene mapping helpers.
nodes.languageNotes: Python modules use FastAPI, async I/O, NumPy/SciPy, and Numba where performance matters.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Marks the optimizer package containing job dispatch and evolutionary trajectory optimizers.

nodes.name: dispatcher.py
nodes.summary: Submits optimization jobs to ARQ, tracks Redis job status, publishes progress, and streams job updates over pub/sub.
nodes.languageNotes: Python modules use FastAPI, async I/O, NumPy/SciPy, and Numba where performance matters.

nodes.name: gmpa.py
nodes.summary: Implements gravity-assist mission candidate generation and optimization request structures for single- and multi-leg searches.
nodes.languageNotes: Python modules use FastAPI, async I/O, NumPy/SciPy, and Numba where performance matters.

nodes.name: multileg_objective.py
nodes.summary: Evaluates multi-leg trajectory objective values against the ephemeris cache and orbital mechanics solver.
nodes.languageNotes: Python modules use FastAPI, async I/O, NumPy/SciPy, and Numba where performance matters.

nodes.name: nsga2.py
nodes.summary: Implements NSGA-II multi-objective evolutionary optimization for transfer time and delta-v tradeoffs.
nodes.languageNotes: Python modules use FastAPI, async I/O, NumPy/SciPy, and Numba where performance matters.

nodes.name: objective.py
nodes.summary: Computes single-leg delta-v objectives and porkchop grid values using ephemeris states and Lambert transfers.
nodes.languageNotes: Python modules use FastAPI, async I/O, NumPy/SciPy, and Numba where performance matters.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Marks the route planner package.

nodes.name: catalog.py
nodes.summary: Defines supported gravity-assist body sequences and nominal orbital periods for planner heuristics.
nodes.languageNotes: Python modules use FastAPI, async I/O, NumPy/SciPy, and Numba where performance matters.

nodes.name: engine.py
nodes.summary: Generates candidate interplanetary routes from catalog sequences, launch windows, ephemeris data, and transfer scoring.
nodes.languageNotes: Python modules use FastAPI, async I/O, NumPy/SciPy, and Numba where performance matters.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Marks the serialization package for binary response encoding.

nodes.name: encoder.py
nodes.summary: Encodes porkchop grids, planetary snapshots, and trajectory responses to protobuf when available, with a manual binary fallback.
nodes.languageNotes: Python modules use FastAPI, async I/O, NumPy/SciPy, and Numba where performance matters.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Marks the ARQ workers package.

nodes.name: worker.py
nodes.summary: Runs background single- and multi-leg optimization jobs, emits progress, and exposes ARQ worker settings.
nodes.languageNotes: Python modules use FastAPI, async I/O, NumPy/SciPy, and Numba where performance matters.

nodes.name: test_api.py
nodes.summary: Provides integration-style API and WebSocket checks covering health, body data, ephemeris, Lambert solving, porkchop, optimization jobs, and live streams.
nodes.languageNotes: Python modules use FastAPI, async I/O, NumPy/SciPy, and Numba where performance matters.

layers.name: Documentation and Configuration
layers.description: Project overview, runtime settings, Python dependencies, and schema contracts that explain or configure the application.

layers.name: Deployment
layers.description: Container and Compose definitions that run Redis, the API, the background worker, and the static frontend.

layers.name: Frontend Experience
layers.description: The browser-based Three.js interface, visual metadata, date utilities, layout, and styling.

layers.name: API and Runtime
layers.description: FastAPI entry point, HTTP and WebSocket routes, runtime settings, binary serialization, and background worker wiring.

layers.name: Ephemeris and Mechanics
layers.description: Celestial-body definitions, Horizons fetching, spline caching, Lambert solving, Kepler propagation, transforms, and multi-leg orbital mechanics.

layers.name: Optimization and Planning
layers.description: Route catalog, planning heuristics, NSGA-II, objective functions, ARQ dispatch helpers, and mission optimization algorithms.

layers.name: Tests and Maintenance
layers.description: API smoke tests plus local patching and ephemeris maintenance scripts.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README to understand the trajectory optimizer's purpose, Docker startup, architecture, and API surface.

tour.title: Runtime Topology
tour.description: Inspect how Docker Compose runs Redis, the FastAPI API, the ARQ worker, and the nginx frontend from the container build.

tour.title: Application Entry Point
tour.description: Follow the FastAPI bootstrap, lifespan cache warmup, CORS setup, and router mounting that bring the backend online.
tour.languageLesson: FastAPI applications often compose routers in the entry point while lifespan hooks handle expensive startup resources.

tour.title: HTTP and WebSocket API
tour.description: Read the request models, validation, REST endpoints, and WebSocket streams that expose ephemeris, transfers, planning, and optimization progress.

tour.title: Ephemeris Cache
tour.description: Understand how body metadata, Horizons fetching, and spline interpolation provide fast planetary state lookups to the API and optimizers.

tour.title: Orbital Mechanics Core
tour.description: Walk through the coordinate transforms, Kepler helpers, Lambert solver, and multi-leg trajectory composition used by transfer calculations.

tour.title: Optimization and Planning
tour.description: Trace how objective functions, NSGA-II, gravity-assist search, route catalog heuristics, and dispatch code turn user requests into candidate missions.

tour.title: Frontend Interaction Loop
tour.description: Inspect the HTML shell, styling, Three.js scene code, planet metadata, and date utilities that render API and WebSocket data for the user.
tour.languageLesson: The browser frontend uses ES modules and async fetch/WebSocket calls to coordinate rendering with backend state.

tour.title: Serialization and Tests
tour.description: Finish with the protobuf contract, binary encoder, ephemeris maintenance script, and API tests that protect the main workflows.
